Istiod logs
- Show normal XDS pushes and initial “pilot_eds_no_instances” for services before endpoints were ready; no persistent errors after workloads came up.

Identified issues and risks
1) Nonstandard gateway in application namespace
   - Evidence: Pod bookinfo/istio-ingressgateway-6d65dbb4f5-htdtx (1/1 Running) exists alongside the control-plane gateway istio-system/istio-ingressgateway-7554995647-bggnd.
   - Risk: Running an ingress gateway inside the app namespace can cause confusion and traffic path duplication. Also, its label sidecar.istio.io/inject=true is set, which is atypical for gateways (gateways usually have injection disabled).
   - Mitigation:
     - If not intentionally using a namespace-scoped gateway, remove the bookinfo/istio-ingressgateway deployment and rely on istio-system/istio-ingressgateway.
     - If you need a namespace-local gateway, ensure sidecar injection is disabled on that deployment (sidecar.istio.io/inject=false) and confirm Service/LoadBalancer exposure and routing are as desired.

2) Default namespace has sidecar injection enabled
   - Evidence: Namespace default labeled istio-injection=enabled.
   - Risk: Unintended workloads in default may get sidecars injected, affecting startup, ports, and network policy.
   - Mitigation: Remove the label from default if injection there is not desired:
     - kubectl label namespace default istio-injection-

3) No DestinationRules defined for bookinfo
   - Evidence: No networking.istio.io/v1beta1 DestinationRule found.
   - Impact: You rely on defaults (load balancing policy, mTLS settings). If you need consistent subset-based routing, outlier detection, or explicit TLS modes, add DestinationRules.

Overall status
- Control plane: Healthy. All critical components in istio-system are Running and Ready. Initial startup warnings resolved.
- Data plane: Healthy. All bookinfo workloads are meshed (2/2) and Ready. Gateway and VirtualService are present; traffic routing should function.
- Action needed: Clean up or correct the extra gateway in bookinfo (if unintentional), and review namespace-wide injection labels and DestinationRules per your policy.
